steps.prompt: How many finite verbs are in this sentence?

hints.One: Look again — both 'Est' and 'sunt' are finite verbs
hints.Three: Only 'Est' and 'sunt' are finite verbs — the rest are nouns, adjectives, and a preposition

hints.to eat: 'to eat' is 'edere' — 'Est' here comes from 'esse' (to be)

hints.sto, stare, steti, statum: These are the principal parts of 'stare' (to stand)
hints.eo, ire, ivi, itum: These are the principal parts of 'ire' (to go)
hints.edo, edere, edi, esum: These are the principal parts of 'edere' (to eat)

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'modus' with -us ending is 2nd declension
hints.3rd: 3rd declension has varied endings — 'modus' is a regular 2nd declension -us noun
hints.4th: 4th declension -us nouns have genitive -us — 'modi' genitive indicates 2nd declension

hints.Nominative: Nominative of 'res' is 'res' — 'rebus' with -bus ending is dative or ablative plural
hints.Dative: Dative plural would also be 'rebus', but 'in' + ablative means 'in' — ablative here
hints.Accusative: Accusative plural of 'res' would be 'res' — 'rebus' is ablative

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'res' with genitive 'rei' is 5th declension
hints.3rd: 3rd declension has genitive -is — 'res' with genitive 'rei' is 5th declension
hints.4th: 4th declension has genitive -us — 'res' with genitive 'rei' is 5th declension

hints.sto, stare, steti, statum: These are the principal parts of 'stare' (to stand)
hints.maneo, manere, mansi, mansum: These are the principal parts of 'manere' (to remain)
hints.videor, videri, visus sum: These are the principal parts of 'videri' (to seem)

hints.Accusative: 'fines' could be accusative plural, but here it is the subject of 'sunt' — nominative
hints.Genitive: Genitive singular of 'finis' would be 'finis' — 'fines' is nominative/accusative plural

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'finis' with genitive 'finis' is 3rd declension
hints.2nd: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um — 'finis' is a 3rd declension i-stem noun
hints.5th: 5th declension nouns end in -es with genitive -ei — 'finis' has genitive 'finis'

translation: There is a measure in things; there are, in short, fixed limits
